title: Assets, Media Admission, Fingerprints, CSS, and Integrity · Behavior Case: Asset Route
summary: Assets are copied by basename, path traversal escapes the output root, CSS references are missed, hashes change with timestamps, huge images ship unchanged, and stale files survive rebuilds. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the asset route increment in bounded content-addressed asset manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Map source asset identity to collision-free public URL and output path without trusting basename or author traversal.
Admit assets by root, type, extension, signature where needed, size, dimensions, license, owner, and active-content policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-assets-fingerprints-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_assets_fingerprints_5(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-asset-route-l0yecu
# Competency: ssg-asset-route.
# Case 4099: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_asset_route_l0yecu(assets: int = 16, admitted: int = 14, rejected: int = 2, missing_rewrites: int = 0, digest_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_l0yecu = "l0yecu-ucey0l-4099"
    assert evidence_variant_l0yecu
    evidence_axes_l0yecu = (
        "readerl0yecu",
        "source4099",
        "parseucey0l",
        "routel0yecu4099",
        "artifact4099l0yecu",
        "recoveryucey0l4099",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_l0yecu)) == 6
    if min(assets, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, assets, "asset-count"
    if admitted + rejected != assets:
        return False, admitted, "asset-reconciliation"
    if missing_rewrites > 0:
        return False, missing_rewrites, "asset-reference"
    if digest_collisions > 0:
        return False, digest_collisions, "asset-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == assets
    return True, admitted, "fingerprinted-assets"

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Route model · Theory Asset Route, keep Asset Route connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map source asset identity to collision-free public URL and output path without trusting basename or author traversal.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Route model · Theory Asset Route, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Asset Route precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Route model · Theory Asset Route, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3980 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit, fingerprint, rewrite, and reconcile bounded licensed assets; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map source asset identity to collision-free public URL and output path without trusting basename or author traversal.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Asset Route model · Theory Asset Route, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Copying assets into one public folder cannot overwrite unrelated files.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-asset-route-l0yecu(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_asset_route_l0yecu\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(assets, admitted, rejected\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted \+ rejected != assets)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?missing_rewrites > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?digest_collisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
